action.reasoning: Observation: Training failed for ~17 iterations (13-29) with persistent ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'xgboost' (packages_to_install was never included in any prior JSON despite model.py/train.py/predict.py being fully ready for XGBRegressor on Morgan FP r=3/2048bit with objective='reg:absoluteerror', tree_method='hist', n_estimators=1000, max_depth=9, lr=0.05, EarlyStopping callback on val, metrics.jsonl logging per RAB_ITERATION, best.pkl save/load). Best MAE remains 0.2188 from RF (iter6); MLP was 0.2314. Iter7 curve shows smooth convergence to ~0.22 plateau but FP+tree ensemble capacity-limited. Analysis: xgboost is explicitly allowed, persists after one pip install; with MAE objective + early_stop on val it should substantially beat RF baseline (~0.15-0.18 MAE expected) on this high-dim sparse tabular QM9 task. FP calc (~4-6min) fits in remaining ~1.76h with 1 iter left. No code bugs (callback fixed, fast ConvertToNumpyArray FP gen, valid imports). Decision: Install xgboost now, run current train.py (full CSV, computes FPs, fits with callback+eval_set, logs val_mae, saves best.pkl), submit_eval on best.pkl for final test MAE improvement.
